_rolesNote: roleMap covers the 12 Ryzeal cards (6 Lv4 monsters, 2 Rank-4 Xyz finishers, 4 Spell/Trap). All Ryzeal Lv4 SS clauses lock the player into Rank-4 Xyz summons for the turn. Detonator + Duo Drive are the canonical endboard Xyz bodies.

goals.name: Detonator + Duo Drive + Cross canonical
goals.description: Fixture-expected Ryzeal endboard: both Rank-4 Xyz finishers on the field + Ryzeal Cross set. Detonator is the quick-effect destroy interruption; Duo Drive is the ATK-swing + hand-pump (draw 2 Ryzeal cards). Cross is the monster-effect-negate trap. Together = 2 interruptions + continuous ATK pressure + card advantage.

successors.note: Phase B (2026-04-21): when the Ryzeal canonical endboard is reached AND the deck contains Feral Imps + Habakiri + Saji + Mitsurugi Ritual, the 2-Lv4 → Mitsurugi ritual body bridge promotes this waypoint into the hybrid apex. The waypoint has already proven ≥4 Lv4 materials flowed through the Ryzeal engine; an additional 2 Lv4 for Feral Imps is realistic when extenders (Node recursion, Plugin, Star detach) are present in the starting hand.

goals.name: 2 Rank-4 Ryzeal Xyz
goals.description: Both Xyz finishers (Detonator + Duo Drive) present, no trap set. Strong disruption even without Cross — Detonator handles 1 field destroy, Duo Drive handles hand pump.

goals.name: Detonator alone
goals.description: Just Ryzeal Detonator on the field — sole quick-effect interruption. Fallback endboard when the combo was short-circuited by handtraps.

goals.name: Duo Drive alone
goals.description: Just Ryzeal Duo Drive on the field — no interruption but +2 draw on activation (immediate card advantage).

goals.name: Any Ryzeal Xyz on field
goals.description: Gradient signal for DFS: any Ryzeal Rank-4 Xyz summoned (Detonator OR Duo Drive).

goals.name: Ryzeal Cross set
goals.description: Gradient: Cross set as backrow, waiting for opp-turn negate window.

goals.name: Seventh Tachyon activated (Xyz-lock engaged)
goals.description: Bridge signal (Lever 3 2026-04-21): Seventh Tachyon in GY means it was activated — reveals a Number 101-107 Xyz, searches Lv-matching Type/Attribute monster (typically Sword Ryzeal Lv4 LIGHT Pyro in this deck's ED). Locks into Xyz-only SS rest of turn — commits the game plan to Rank-4 tower. Strong progress signal the Ryzeal engine has engaged via the Number-Xyz pathway rather than direct Lv4 starter chain.

required.note: Seventh Tachyon used (in GY after resolution)

goals.name: Bonfire used (Pyro search)
goals.description: Progress signal: Bonfire in GY = searched Lv4-or-lower Pyro (Ice Ryzeal in this deck). Confirms the Ryzeal engine has at least 1 searchable starter unlocked — a distinct entry point vs. drawing the starter naturally.

routes.name: Ice NS → Sword SS → Xyz Detonator → Xyz Duo Drive (2-Xyz tower)

steps.note: NS Ice Ryzeal — triggers on-NS: SS a Ryzeal (non-Ice) from deck
SS Sword Ryzeal — from hand via Ryzeal-in-field condition, OR from deck via Ice on-NS
Sword on-NS/SS: search LIGHT Pyro (any Ryzeal extender)
Xyz Summon Detonator using 2 Lv4 Ryzeal materials
SS 3rd/4th Ryzeal (searched / recycled via Node / Plugin) for 2nd Xyz materials
Xyz Summon Duo Drive using remaining 2 Lv4 Ryzeal materials
Duo Drive main-phase effect: detach 2 materials → draw 2 Ryzeal cards from deck (target Star + Cross)

routes.name: Ice NS → SS Ryzeal from deck → Xyz material

steps.note: NS Ice Ryzeal (Lv4); on-NS: SS 1 Ryzeal from deck (target Sword/Node/Star per context)
Xyz Summon Detonator using the 2 Ryzeal Lv4 as materials
Sword Ryzeal SS from hand (requires Ryzeal in field/GY as a condition). Locks into Rank-4 Xyz.
Sword on-NS/SS: add 1 LIGHT Pyro from deck to hand (typically another Ryzeal)

routes.name: Ext NS (solo field) → search FIRE Thunder

steps.note: NS Ext Ryzeal; on-NS (solo field or Lv/Rank 4 only): search 1 FIRE Thunder from deck

routes.name: Star SS (detach from Xyz) → Set Ryzeal Cross

steps.note: Star Ryzeal SS by detaching 1 Xyz material (needs prior Xyz on field). Locks into Rank-4 Xyz.
Star on-SS: Set Ryzeal Cross from deck (canonical endboard trap)
Duo Drive main-phase: detach 2 materials from any monster(s) you control; add 2 Ryzeal cards (different names) from deck to hand. Hand pump that refills combo resources.

synergies.effect: Ice + Sword = canonical 2-card Rank-4 opener. Ice NS → SS Sword from deck (or vice versa if Sword SS conditions met) → Xyz Summon. Most consistent baseline combo.
Detonator + Duo Drive = endboard 2-card tower. Both have on-SS attach-from-GY (regenerates their own Xyz materials) so subsequent turns remain stocked.
Node Ryzeal recycles Ryzeal from GY (defense position, effects negated — acts as pure material). Combined with Duo Drive's on-SS attach, this extends Rank-4 material pool across turns.
Star Ryzeal's on-SS Set-from-deck is the cleanest way to get Cross onto the backrow without using a Normal Summon or spell activation slot.
Plugin recycles banished/GY Xyz monsters back to the field. Combined with Detonator's destruction-by-detach, Plugin extends the removal engine by bringing back detached Detonators from GY.

Palm Ryzeal is a trap card for this expertise: its hard summon condition (shuffle a Ryzeal from hand/GY INTO the deck) trades card advantage for a body. Avoid as starter — use only as a late-turn follow-up or to recycle a bricked piece.

sourceNotes: v1 (2026-04-21) — written from cards.cdb oracle text. 12 Ryzeal cards mapped. 6 goals: canonical 3-card endboard, 2-Xyz tower, solo Detonator/Duo Drive fallbacks, partial gradients for any Xyz or Cross-set alone. 5 routes covering Ice-opener, Sword-conditional, Ext-solo, Star-set, Duo-Drive hand-pump. Palm Ryzeal flagged as anti-pattern (negative-tempo summon condition). Ryzeal's 'lock into Rank 4 Xyz' is encoded implicitly by goal design (targets specifically Detonator/Duo Drive, both Rank 4).
